content.text: Billing Profiles & Invoice Sections

Under a Microsoft Customer Agreement (MCA), the billing account delegates cost ownership through two sub-levels: billing profiles and invoice sections. A billing profile owns a payment method and generates one invoice per billing period. Invoice sections subdivide a billing profile so different teams or cost centers receive separate line items on the same invoice — without requiring separate subscriptions under separate billing accounts.
This three-tier MCA model (billing account → billing profile → invoice section) is the primary mechanism for multi-department cost segregation in modern Azure commercial agreements.

attrs.alt: MCA billing hierarchy: account, profiles, invoice sections, and subscriptions

content.text: Billing Profiles in Depth

Each billing profile represents one independent billing relationship: its own payment method (credit card, wire transfer, check), billing address, currency, and invoice cycle. Subscriptions linked through invoice sections inherit the billing profile's currency and payment method.
Attribute
Scope
Notes
Payment method
Per billing profile
Credit card, bank transfer, or check — set once, applies to all subscriptions
Currency
Per billing profile
Locked at creation; cannot be changed after first invoice
Invoice day
Per billing profile
First of month by default; can be changed to a specific day
Invoice email
Per billing profile
One or more email addresses that receive the PDF invoice
Spending limit
Per billing profile
Optional cap; resets monthly or at specified intervals
Azure credits
Per billing profile
MACC and trial credits apply at this level
Listing Billing Profiles

# List billing profiles for an MCA billing account
az billing profile list \
  --account-name "<billing-account-id>" \
  --output table

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

billing_account_name = "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx:xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx_2019-05-31"

url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/billingProfiles"
    f"?api-version=2020-05-01"
)
resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers)
for profile in resp.json().get("value", []):
    props = profile.get("properties", {})
    print(
        f"Name: {profile['name']}  "
        f"DisplayName: {props.get('displayName')}  "
        f"Currency: {props.get('currency')}  "
        f"Status: {props.get('status')}"
    )
Invoice Sections in Depth

Invoice sections group subscriptions within a billing profile for line-item cost separation. Each invoice section appears as a separate section on the billing profile's invoice. Roles assigned at the invoice section level grant access only to that section's cost data — a department manager can see Engineering's costs without seeing Marketing's.
Creating an Invoice Section

az billing invoice section create \
  --account-name "<billing-account-id>" \
  --profile-name "<billing-profile-id>" \
  --invoice-section-name "Engineering" \
  --display-name "Engineering Department"
Listing Invoice Sections and Their Subscriptions

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

billing_account_name = "<billing-account-id>"
billing_profile_name = "<billing-profile-id>"

# List invoice sections
sections_url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
    f"invoiceSections?api-version=2020-05-01&$expand=billingSubscriptions"
)
resp = requests.get(sections_url, headers=headers)
for section in resp.json().get("value", []):
    props = section.get("properties", {})
    subs = props.get("billingSubscriptions", [])
    print(f"Section: {props.get('displayName')} ({section['name']}) — {len(subs)} subscriptions")
    for sub in subs:
        sub_props = sub.get("properties", {})
        print(f"  {sub_props.get('displayName')} ({sub_props.get('subscriptionId')})")
Access Control: RBAC at Billing Profile and Invoice Section Levels

Billing RBAC roles are distinct from Azure RBAC roles — they control who can read invoices, manage payment methods, and create subscriptions, not who can create resources. Roles are granted separately at the billing profile and invoice section levels.
Role
Scope
Capabilities
Billing Profile Owner
Billing profile
Full control: edit profile, view invoices, manage payment, create invoice sections
Billing Profile Contributor
Billing profile
Edit profile settings; cannot manage payment methods
Billing Profile Reader
Billing profile
Read-only: view invoice and cost data
Invoice Section Owner
Invoice section
Create subscriptions, assign invoice section roles
Invoice Section Contributor
Invoice section
Create subscriptions; cannot manage roles
Invoice Section Reader
Invoice section
Read cost data for this section only
Azure Subscription Creator
Invoice section
Create subscriptions only; no cost visibility
Assigning Billing Roles via Python

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

billing_account_name = "<billing-account-id>"
billing_profile_name = "<billing-profile-id>"
invoice_section_name = "engineering-dept"

# Grant Invoice Section Owner to a user
# Role definition IDs are stable GUIDs per role type
INVOICE_SECTION_OWNER = "da6647fb-7651-49ee-be91-c43c4877f0c4"

user_object_id = "<aad-object-id-of-user>"

url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
    f"invoiceSections/{invoice_section_name}/"
    f"createBillingRoleAssignment?api-version=2020-05-01"
)

body = {
    "properties": {
        "principalId": user_object_id,
        "principalTenantId": "<tenant-id>",
        "roleDefinitionId": (
            f"/providers/Microsoft.Billing/billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
            f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
            f"invoiceSections/{invoice_section_name}/"
            f"billingRoleDefinitions/{INVOICE_SECTION_OWNER}"
        )
    }
}

resp = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=body)
print(resp.status_code, resp.json())
Moving a Subscription Between Invoice Sections

Subscriptions can be transferred between invoice sections within the same billing profile (and between profiles on the same billing account) without interrupting resources. The subscription keeps its resource group and resources; only the cost attribution changes.

# Transfer a subscription to a different invoice section
az billing subscription transfer \
  --account-name "<billing-account-id>" \
  --profile-name "<target-billing-profile-id>" \
  --invoice-section-name "<target-invoice-section-id>" \
  --subscription-billing-name "<subscription-id>"

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

billing_account_name = "<billing-account-id>"
subscription_id = "<subscription-id>"

# Initiate transfer to a different invoice section
url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingSubscriptions/{subscription_id}/"
    f"move?api-version=2020-05-01"
)

body = {
    "destinationInvoiceSectionId": (
        f"/providers/Microsoft.Billing/billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"billingProfiles/<target-profile-id>/"
        f"invoiceSections/<target-section-id>"
    )
}

resp = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=body)
if resp.status_code == 202:
    # Long-running operation — poll the Location header
    import time
    location = resp.headers.get("Location")
    while True:
        poll = requests.get(location, headers=headers)
        if poll.status_code == 200:
            print("Transfer complete:", poll.json().get("status"))
            break
        time.sleep(5)
else:
    print(resp.status_code, resp.json())
Subscription moves between invoice sections take effect immediately for new charges. Historical charges remain attributed to the original section in that billing period's invoice.
Cost Analysis Scoped to Invoice Sections
Azure Cost Management supports cost queries scoped to a billing profile or invoice section, enabling per-department reports without requiring separate billing accounts.
Automating Billing Profile and Invoice Section Provisioning

Enterprise Landing Zone patterns provision billing profiles and invoice sections as part of the subscription vending workflow. The pattern below ties together Terraform for resource provisioning and the Azure Billing REST API for billing hierarchy setup.
Terraform: Billing Profile Data Source
Fully Automated Invoice Section Provisioning Script
Spending Limit and Credit Management on Billing Profiles

MCA billing profiles support a spending limit that prevents overage if Azure credits are exhausted. When the limit is reached, new resource deployments are blocked but existing resources continue running.
Checking and Updating Spending Limits

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

billing_account_name = "<billing-account-id>"
billing_profile_name = "<billing-profile-id>"

profile_url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}"
    f"?api-version=2020-05-01"
)
resp = requests.get(profile_url, headers=headers)
props = resp.json().get("properties", {})
print("Spending limit:", props.get("spendingLimit"))      # Active / Off / Optional
print("Status:",         props.get("status"))             # Active / Disabled / Warned
print("Currency:",       props.get("currency"))
print("Invoice day:",    props.get("invoiceDay"))

# Disable spending limit (requires Billing Profile Owner)
patch_body = {
    "properties": {
        "spendingLimit": "Off"
    }
}
patch_resp = requests.patch(profile_url, headers=headers, json=patch_body)
print("Update status:", patch_resp.status_code)
Listing Azure Credits on a Billing Profile

import requests
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

billing_account_name = "<billing-account-id>"
billing_profile_name = "<billing-profile-id>"

credits_url = (
    f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
    f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
    f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}/"
    f"availableBalance?api-version=2020-05-01"
)
resp = requests.get(credits_url, headers=headers)
balance = resp.json().get("properties", {})
print(f"Available Credit Balance: {balance.get('amount', {}).get('value', 0):.2f} {balance.get('amount', {}).get('currency', '')}")
Bulk Invoice Section Inventory and Cost Report

Operations teams routinely need a cross-section cost breakdown showing all invoice sections, their subscriptions, and month-to-date spend. The following script generates a CSV suitable for chargeback reporting.

import requests
import csv
import sys
from datetime import datetime
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential

def get_section_costs(billing_account_name: str, billing_profile_name: str, output_csv: str):
    credential = DefaultAzureCredential()
    token = credential.get_token("https://management.azure.com/.default").token
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    }

    base = (
        f"https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Billing/"
        f"billingAccounts/{billing_account_name}/"
        f"billingProfiles/{billing_profile_name}"
    )

    # Get all invoice sections
    sections_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base}/invoiceSections?api-version=2020-05-01",
        headers=headers
    )
    sections = sections_resp.json().get("value", [])

    rows = []
    for section in sections:
        section_name = section["name"]
        section_display = section["properties"]["displayName"]
        labels = section["properties"].get("labels", {})

        # Query month-to-date cost for this section
        scope = f"{base}/invoiceSections/{section_name}"
        query_url = f"https://management.azure.com{scope}/providers/Microsoft.CostManagement/query?api-version=2023-11-01"
        query_body = {
            "type": "ActualCost",
            "timeframe": "MonthToDate",
            "dataSet": {
                "granularity": "None",
                "aggregation": {
                    "totalCost": {"name": "Cost", "function": "Sum"}
                }
            }
        }
        cost_resp = requests.post(query_url, headers=headers, json=query_body)
        cost_data = cost_resp.json()
        cost_rows = cost_data.get("properties", {}).get("rows", [])
        total_cost = cost_rows[0][0] if cost_rows else 0.0

        rows.append({
            "billing_profile": billing_profile_name,
            "invoice_section": section_name,
            "display_name": section_display,
            "cost_center": labels.get("cost-center", ""),
            "department": labels.get("department", ""),
            "mtd_cost_usd": round(total_cost, 2),
            "report_date": datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y-%m-%d"),
        })

    with open(output_csv, "w", newline="") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=rows[0].keys() if rows else [])
        writer.writeheader()
        writer.writerows(rows)

    print(f"Written {len(rows)} sections to {output_csv}")
    total = sum(r["mtd_cost_usd"] for r in rows)
    print(f"Total MTD cost: {total:,.2f} USD")

get_section_costs("<billing-account-id>", "<billing-profile-id>", "section_costs.csv")
Common Pitfalls and Operational Notes
Pitfall
Impact
Mitigation
Currency locked at profile creation
Cannot change after first invoice; cross-currency reporting requires manual FX conversion
Choose currency carefully before creating first subscription
Billing RBAC ≠ Azure RBAC
Billing Profile Reader cannot see Azure resources; Azure Owner cannot see invoices
Assign billing roles explicitly for finance teams alongside resource roles
Spending limit blocks deployments silently
New resource creates fail with quota-like errors when credit exhausted
Set budget alerts before the spending limit triggers; disable the limit for production profiles
Invoice section name is immutable
Display name can change; the resource name (used in ARM IDs) cannot
Use a stable naming convention (cc-1001, dept-eng) for invoice section names
Subscription move changes cost attribution immediately
Invoices for a partial month split across old and new sections
Move subscriptions at month boundaries when possible to simplify reconciliation
Summary

Billing profiles and invoice sections are the two sub-levels of the MCA billing hierarchy below the billing account. A billing profile owns the payment method and generates one invoice; invoice sections partition that invoice by team or cost center. Billing RBAC roles at each level independently control who can view and manage costs, keeping financial data segmented without requiring separate billing accounts.
Billing profiles: own payment method, currency, and invoice — cannot be shared across billing accounts
Invoice sections: cost center groupings within a profile — appear as separate sections on the monthly invoice
RBAC is additive and independent: Invoice Section Owner does not grant Billing Profile Reader
Subscription moves between invoice sections take effect immediately for new charges only
Cost queries can be scoped directly to an invoice section for per-department reporting
